name: ntulearn-mcp
description: MCP server for NTULearn (NTU Singapore's Blackboard Learn). Read your courses, content, announcements, and grades.
long_description:
Lets Claude read your NTULearn courses, content, announcements, and grades. Authenticates via the BbRouter cookie — auto-read from your browser on macOS / Linux / Windows-with-Firefox; Windows + Chrome/Edge users provide it manually via the configuration field below (Claude Desktop stores it in your OS keychain).

Tools: list_courses, get_course_contents, get_folder_children, get_announcements, get_gradebook, search_course_content, get_file_download_url, download_file, read_file_content (extracts text from PDFs / Office files inline so Claude can answer questions about lecture material directly).

bbrouter_cookie.title: BbRouter cookie (optional)
bbrouter_cookie.description: Leave empty to auto-read from your browser. Required on Windows + Chrome/Edge (browser auto-read is blocked there). To get the value: log into ntulearn.ntu.edu.sg, open DevTools → Application → Cookies → ntulearn.ntu.edu.sg, copy the BbRouter cookie value.

tools.name: ntulearn_list_courses
tools.description: List the user's enrolled NTULearn courses.

tools.name: ntulearn_get_course_contents
tools.description: Get the content tree of a course (folders, items, links).

tools.name: ntulearn_get_folder_children
tools.description: List the children of a content folder.

tools.name: ntulearn_get_announcements
tools.description: Get announcements for a course or across all courses.

tools.name: ntulearn_get_gradebook
tools.description: Get the gradebook (grade columns + grades) for a course.

tools.name: ntulearn_search_course_content
tools.description: Full-text search across a course's content.

tools.name: ntulearn_get_file_download_url
tools.description: Resolve the authenticated download URL for a content file.

tools.name: ntulearn_download_file
tools.description: Download a content file to the local filesystem.

tools.name: ntulearn_read_file_content
tools.description: Fetch a content file and extract text inline (PDF / docx / pptx / xlsx) so Claude can answer questions about it without a filesystem hop.
